design_philosophy.core_loop: Traverse themed worlds, collect puzzle cards from NPC houses, throw blocks at enemies, unlock progression by assembling card sets
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Exploration vs. combat—players must navigate levels to find hidden cards while managing enemy encounters with limited throwing mechanics
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Adventure as two kids in a whimsical McDonaldland, collecting magical artifacts and outwitting the Hamburglar
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily mechanics-driven with novel physics systems (gravity spinner, zippered warps, rideable boats). Puzzle card gating over traditional level progression. Block-throwing combat replaces standard jumping-based enemy defeat. Success requires synthesis of exploration and collection, not just level completion.

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives counter with secret infinite lives function (bugged, requires collecting all Arches in three stages then dying)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden secret completion mechanic (infinite lives via Arches) suggests optional difficulty reduction for dedicated players, though implementation is flawed

progression.upgrade_system: Puzzle card collection system—players gather themed card sets from each world's NPC character (Ronald: 4 cards, Birdie: 5 cards, Grimace: 3 cards, Professor: 5 cards, CosMc: 5 cards, Hamburglar: 6 cards) to advance plot and access new areas
progression.unlock_mechanism: Card collection gates progression to next world. Each NPC requires specific card count before revealing villain location or next destination
replayability_hooks: Hidden infinite lives function (though buggy implementation discourages discovery)
Two playable characters with cosmetic differences

world_building_techniques: Character visits at world start (Ronald, Birdie, Grimace, Professor, CosMc)
Storybook framing device (opening cutscene establishing Hamburglar theft)
NPC dialogue revealing villain location progression
Themed worlds reflecting McDonaldland franchise imagery (treehouse, highlands, moon, volcano)
Licensed character integration (Ronald McDonald, Hamburglar, Grimace, Birdie, CosMc)

boss_design.philosophy: Thematic final encounter based on corrupted item rather than traditional villain. Magic Bag itself becomes the antagonist, attacking with abstract magical projectiles (tied flag, wand, rabbit-in-hat)
notable_bosses: The Magic Bag (final boss)—launches multiple projectile attacks; defeated by collecting six Hamburglar cards and confronting it directly

faction_indicators: McDonaldland characters represent non-hostile factions (Ronald, Birdie, Grimace, Professor, CosMc)
danger_signals: Hamburglar's presence in world signals escalating threat
Stolen Magic Bag represents corrupted power source
Volcano world represents final danger zone before confrontation
environmental_storytelling: Progression through distinct themed zones (clubhouse → treehouse → highlands → workshop → moon → volcano) mirrors adventure intensity escalation
Each NPC house contains collectibles reflecting their character identity
Rocket gift from Professor marks technological progression and escalation
Volcano setting as natural final confrontation space
